methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
ADASI is a sovereign-funded frontier-tech company in the Defense & Security sector at the EDGE stage, backed by the EDGE Group. The valuation universe comprises 10 comparable companies: 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private competitors including Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems.

Peer-median valuation analysis was not applicable due to insufficient narrow-stage comparability. Of 19 same-sector peers identified, none satisfied simultaneous filters for stage alignment, adjacent-stage positioning, or similar valuation tier—the minimum threshold for reliable peer triangulation is 3 datapoints. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the specialized capital intensity and sovereign funding dynamics of Defense & Security.

The analysis framework identifies the comp universe but cannot support a triangulated valuation range without additional data. Key uncertainties include: (1) the stage-specific valuation multiples for sovereign-backed defense entities; (2) disclosure limitations among private comparables; and (3) the heterogeneity of business models and technology maturity across the peer set. To enable robust valuation, future analysis requires either proprietary transaction data from recent Defense & Security financings or a relaxation of stage filters with documented justification for cross-stage comparisons. Current data support identification of relevant comps but not quantitative synthesis into a defensible range.
